title: Instructional Resources – Instructional Materials
description: This policy governs how the school district selects and approves the textbooks, digital resources, and other instructional materials used in classrooms. It applies to district staff, curriculum leaders, and the school board, but directly affects students and the families who rely on quality, vetted materials for their children's education. Parents and community members should be aware of this policy because it outlines the process for ensuring that what is taught in schools meets educational standards and has been reviewed and approved before reaching students.

timeline.summary: This is a draft presentation slide (page 82) describing the District Selection Process for instructional materials as required by Board Policy EFA. It explains that the district will form a representative committee of teachers, campus instructional coaches, principals, and other district-level staff to evaluate instructional materials from the state's approved list and make a recommendation to the Board. This is not a redline policy document — it is a procedural overview slide referencing EFA.
timeline.discussion: policy EM (teaching about controversial issues). Raised two specific concerns: (1) the addition of "unpatriotic or anti-American sentiments" as prohibited — terms he called undefined and ambiguous; (2) a new "prohibited terms" section banning "Latinx" and "minor attracted person," which he opposed. He urged the board to give these revisions appropriate scrutiny.
timeline.notes: This slide is labeled 'DRAFT' and is a presentation page, not a redline policy document. It references Policy EFA but contains no proposed policy language additions or deletions. It describes the committee-based district selection process for instructional materials. The slide appears to be part of a broader board presentation packet, likely an informational item accompanying a curriculum/materials adoption discussion.
